Describe carbon cycle processes: photosynthesis, respiration, feeding, decomposition, fossil fuels, combustion
Identify the Sun as the primary energy source for biological systems.
Explain how cations and anions form through electron transfer.
Define food chains as energy transfer pathways starting with producers
Distinguish between physical and chemical changes and explain their differences
Redox reactions involve simultaneous oxidation and reduction processes.
Define seven key characteristics that distinguish living organisms from non-living matter.
Compare plant and animal cell structures including organelles
Understand the Sun's proximity and light-year distance measurement
Describe Solar System structure: Sun, planets, minor planets, dwarf planets, asteroids, moons
State and apply the magnification formula relating image and actual sizes.
State definition of covalent bonds and electron sharing leading to noble gas configurations
Define diffusion as particle movement down concentration gradients via random motion.
Define ionising nuclear radiation and background radiation.
Define osmosis as water diffusion through partially permeable membranes.
Explain that nuclei consist of protons and neutrons
Describe active transport as movement against concentration gradient using respiration energy

Define isotopes: same element, same protons, different neutrons.
